method: Per-result combined: keyword_overlap (≥2 → forced kw hit), semantic cosine (≥0.7 → forced sem hit), functional judge (≥0.7 → forced fn hit). Final hit = any of the three.

results.title: KVTuner
results.reason: Layer-wise budget allocation but applied to quantization bits; no slope-coupled frontier; all signals below thresholds.

results.title: OBCache
results.reason: Token-level saliency pruning with survivor-budget concentration; functional analog of self-thinning. Judge >= 0.7.

results.title: DepthKV
results.reason: Fixed global memory constraint, per-layer dynamic budget allocation. Strongest functional match.

results.title: The Sparse Frontier
results.reason: 'Frontier' framing matches; Pareto trade-off curve = candidate's constraint frontier. Both semantic and functional above threshold.

results.title: Expected Attention
results.reason: Reallocation framing present but at token-level only; below 0.7 on judge.

results.title: Making Every Head Count
results.reason: Surviving heads carry concentrated load along trade-off curve — direct conceptual match. Forced by both keyword (2 overlaps) and functional judge.

results.title: Specialized Heads Do the Heavy Lifting
results.reason: Foundational head-pruning + specialization result; all three signals above threshold.

results.title: Efficient self-attention with smart pruning
results.reason: Smart pruning preserves surviving head capacity; functional hit.

verdict: FAIL — dense prior art exists for transformer self-thinning attention: DepthKV (fixed budget + layer allocation along constraint frontier), Sparse Frontier (explicit Pareto frontier framing), Making Every Head Count (surviving-head load concentration), Voita 2019 (head specialization after pruning). The Reineke slope-1.6 constraint is not yet in print but is a small parametric specialization of well-trodden ground.
